note_data.title: The nuclear atom
keyPoints: **Atoms** consist of a small, dense nucleus surrounded by electrons in shells or energy levels
The **nucleus** contains protons and neutrons and is positively charged due to the protons
**Protons** have a positive charge (+1) and relative mass of 1, located in the nucleus
**Neutrons** have no charge (neutral) and relative mass of 1, located in the nucleus
**Electrons** have a negative charge (-1) and negligible mass, orbiting the nucleus in shells
The **atomic number** (Z) equals the number of protons in the nucleus
The **mass number** (A) equals the total number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus
**Isotopes** are atoms of the same element with the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons
Atoms are **electrically neutral** because the number of protons equals the number of electrons
The diameter of the nucleus is about 10,000 times smaller than the diameter of the atom

definitions.definition: The small, dense, positively charged center of an atom containing protons and neutrons
The number of protons in the nucleus of an atom
The total number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus of an atom
Atoms of the same element that have the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons
An atom that has gained or lost electrons and therefore has a net electric charge

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give detailed account of structure or properties
State similarities AND differences between particles/atoms

modelAnswer.question: An atom of carbon-12 has 6 protons. State the number of neutrons and electrons in this atom. Explain why the atom is electrically neutral.
modelAnswer.answer: Number of neutrons = 6. Number of electrons = 6. The atom is electrically neutral because the number of positively charged protons equals the number of negatively charged electrons, so the charges cancel out.
markBreakdown: Mark 3: Equal number of protons and electrons

commonMistakes: Never say electrons are 'in' the nucleus — they orbit around it in shells
Students often confuse mass number with atomic number — mass number includes neutrons
Never say atoms have 'no mass' — they have very small mass, mostly in the nucleus
Don't forget that ions have unequal numbers of protons and electrons
Students incorrectly think neutrons have negative charge — they are neutral (no charge)
examinerTips: This topic appears in every paper — learn the charges and masses of particles
Always show working when calculating neutrons: neutrons = mass number - atomic number
Draw clear atomic structure diagrams with nucleus and electron shells labeled
Use notation ᴬₓX where A is mass number, Z is atomic number, X is element symbol
formulas: Number of neutrons = Mass number - Atomic number
Mass number = Number of protons + Number of neutrons
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Atomic structure showing small central nucleus containing protons (+) and neutrons (n), with electrons (-) in circular shells around the nucleus. Label nucleus, protons, neutrons, electrons, and electron shells.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P1 Q12 (1 mark) — they asked students to estimate the number of atoms in a mobile phone handset by dividing handset volume by atom volume
This topic appeared in O/N 2022 P2 Q4 (3 marks) — candidates had to explain thermal energy transfer through metal cylinder walls in terms of electrons and atoms
This concept was examined in M/J 2024 P1 Q8 (1 mark) — students needed to select the best estimate of atoms in 50 cm³ of metal
This topic was tested in O/N 2023 P2 Q5 (4 marks) — they asked about crystal atoms at cube corners with given mass and density to find shortest distance between adjacent atoms

workedExample.question: Crystal atoms are positioned at the corners of cubes. Each atom has a mass of 3.5×10⁻²⁵ kg and the density of the crystal is 9.2×10³ kg/m³. Calculate the shortest distance between the centers of adjacent atoms. (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Find volume per atom using V = m/ρ = (3.5×10⁻²⁵ kg)/(9.2×10³ kg/m³)
Step 3: Since atoms are at cube corners, each cube contains 1 atom (8 corners × 1/8 atom per corner)
Step 4: Volume of cube = volume per atom = 3.8×10⁻²⁹ m³
Step 5: Side length of cube = ∛(3.8×10⁻²⁹) = 3.4×10⁻¹⁰ m
workedExample.answer: The shortest distance between adjacent atoms is 3.4×10⁻¹⁰ m
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct formula V=m/ρ, 1 mark for substitution, 1 mark for recognizing cube geometry, 1 mark for correct final answer with units

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused atomic radius with atomic diameter when estimating atomic sizes' — always check whether the question asks for radius or diameter
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often forgot to account for shared atoms in crystal structures' — remember that corner atoms are shared between 8 adjacent cubes
Examiner Report 2024: 'Candidates frequently gave answers without appropriate units or to incorrect significant figures' — always include units and match significant figures to the given data
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw Rutherford's nuclear model showing: a tiny, dense, positively charged nucleus at the center (labeled +) surrounded by mostly empty space with electrons (shown as small circles with - signs) orbiting at relatively large distances. Include scale indication showing nucleus is ~10⁻¹⁵ m while atom is ~10⁻¹⁰ m. Label the nucleus, electrons, and empty space clearly.
note_data.quickRecap: Atoms consist of a tiny, dense, positively charged nucleus surrounded by electrons in mostly empty space. The nucleus is ~10⁻¹⁵ m while the whole atom is ~10⁻¹⁰ m. Most of an atom's mass is concentrated in the nucleus. Typical atomic spacing in solids is ~10⁻¹⁰ m, and a small volume of matter contains ~10²³ atoms.
